content:
# playwright-locator-best-practices - Overview

Automation type: browser_playwright
Tooling: Playwright
Goal: Use resilient locators and avoid brittle selectors in browser automation.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://playwright.dev/docs/best-practices

When to use:
- Automating web UI
- Selectors are flaky
- Agent needs to click/fill stable elements

Inputs required:
- Target URL
- User-visible labels/roles
- Stable test ids if available

AI summary: Use Playwright resilient locators: role, label, text, placeholder, test id, and scoped locators. Avoid brittle CSS/XPath and arbitrary sleeps.

# playwright-locator-best-practices - Execution Steps

Automation type: browser_playwright
Tooling: Playwright
Goal: Use resilient locators and avoid brittle selectors in browser automation.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://playwright.dev/docs/best-practices

- Prefer role, text, label, placeholder, and test id locators over CSS/XPath.
- Use Playwright Inspector/codegen only as a starting point; review locator semantics.
- Scope locators to a region when text repeats.
- Avoid selecting by dynamic class names or layout-dependent nth-child selectors.

AI summary: Use Playwright resilient locators: role, label, text, placeholder, test id, and scoped locators. Avoid brittle CSS/XPath and arbitrary sleeps.

# playwright-locator-best-practices - Commands or APIs

Automation type: browser_playwright
Tooling: Playwright
Goal: Use resilient locators and avoid brittle selectors in browser automation.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://playwright.dev/docs/best-practices

- page.getByRole(...)
- page.getByLabel(...)
- page.getByText(...)
- locator.filter(...)

AI summary: Use Playwright resilient locators: role, label, text, placeholder, test id, and scoped locators. Avoid brittle CSS/XPath and arbitrary sleeps.

# playwright-locator-best-practices - Verification

Automation type: browser_playwright
Tooling: Playwright
Goal: Use resilient locators and avoid brittle selectors in browser automation.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://playwright.dev/docs/best-practices

- Element action succeeds without arbitrary sleep
- Locator remains unique or intentionally scoped
- Screenshot or assertion confirms result

AI summary: Use Playwright resilient locators: role, label, text, placeholder, test id, and scoped locators. Avoid brittle CSS/XPath and arbitrary sleeps.

# playwright-locator-best-practices - Failure Modes

Automation type: browser_playwright
Tooling: Playwright
Goal: Use resilient locators and avoid brittle selectors in browser automation.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://playwright.dev/docs/best-practices

- Multiple matching elements
- Text changes due to locale
- Dynamic class/hash changes
- Hidden duplicate element

AI summary: Use Playwright resilient locators: role, label, text, placeholder, test id, and scoped locators. Avoid brittle CSS/XPath and arbitrary sleeps.

# playwright-locator-best-practices - Safety Notes

Automation type: browser_playwright
Tooling: Playwright
Goal: Use resilient locators and avoid brittle selectors in browser automation.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://playwright.dev/docs/best-practices

- Do not automate destructive actions without explicit user instruction.
- Prefer assertions before submit/publish/delete.

AI summary: Use Playwright resilient locators: role, label, text, placeholder, test id, and scoped locators. Avoid brittle CSS/XPath and arbitrary sleeps.

# playwright-locator-best-practices - Handoff Report

Automation type: browser_playwright
Tooling: Playwright
Goal: Use resilient locators and avoid brittle selectors in browser automation.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://playwright.dev/docs/best-practices

- Selectors used
- Why they are stable
- Screenshots/assertions

AI summary: Use Playwright resilient locators: role, label, text, placeholder, test id, and scoped locators. Avoid brittle CSS/XPath and arbitrary sleeps.
